meta.destination: Grand Canyon National Park, South Rim

arrival.drive_to_base: ~4 hrs to Page, AZ first; ~3.5 hrs Page to Tusayan

meta.base_camp_1: Page, AZ (Days 0–3) — gateway to canyon country: Horseshoe Bend, Antelope Canyon, Lake Powell
meta.base_camp_2: Tusayan, AZ (Days 4–6) — gateway to Grand Canyon South Rim
meta.critical_context: This is a REGIONAL canyon country trip, not purely a Grand Canyon trip. The human spent more time in Page/canyon country than at Grand Canyon itself. An AI testing only 'Grand Canyon' will miss this entirely — that is expected and informative.
meta.notes: Antelope Canyon requires a guided Navajo tour — walk-up not allowed. Rainbow Bridge requires a full-day boat tour from Lake Powell Marina. Helicopter ride was planned but not executed.

locations_visited.name: Glen Canyon Dam
locations_visited.note: Half-day. The dam and Horseshoe Canyon viewpoint. Can be combined with other Page activities.

locations_visited.name: Horseshoe Bend
locations_visited.note: 8 AM guided tour. Mile Marker 545, Highway 89. Short walk from parking — 20-30 min at the overlook. Early morning for best light and fewer crowds.

locations_visited.name: Antelope Canyon (Lower or Upper)
locations_visited.note: 2:20 PM Navajo guided tour — MUST be booked in advance (navajoantelopecanyon.com). Upper for shafts of light, Lower for sculpted waves. Both require guides.

locations_visited.name: Lake Powell — Rainbow Bridge National Monument
locations_visited.note: 7 AM or earlier departure. Full-day boat tour. Rainbow Bridge is the world's largest natural bridge. This is NOT a half-day activity.

locations_visited.name: Grand Canyon South Rim — Hermit Road viewpoints
locations_visited.note: 9 AM start. Drive Hermit Road end-to-end, stop at all viewpoints: Trailview, Maricopa, Powell, Hopi, Mohave, Pima, Hermits Rest. This is the definitive South Rim experience.

locations_visited.name: Grand Canyon Rim Trail
locations_visited.note: 9 AM departure morning. Easy paved trail along the rim. 2-3 hours. Last activity before driving to Phoenix.

locations_notably_excluded.name: Grand Canyon West Rim / Skywalk
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Crossed out — too commercialized, 2-3 hrs detour, not worth it vs. South Rim

locations_notably_excluded.name: Hoover Dam
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Crossed out — not worth the detour given time constraints

locations_notably_excluded.name: Grand Canyon North Rim
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Crossed out — requires a full separate day trip, too far from South Rim base

locations_notably_excluded.name: Bright Angel Trail (full)
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Crossed out — likely too strenuous given the schedule. Rim Trail is the substitute.

locations_notably_excluded.name: Helicopter ride
locations_notably_excluded.reason: Was planned for Day 4 transit day but marked 'Not Started' — not executed

days.title: Fly Phoenix → Drive to Page
days.theme: Travel day. Land, pick up car, drive to Page. Grocery stop en route.

rows.notes: Stock up — Page has limited options

days.title: Page Orientation — Glen Canyon Dam
days.theme: Easy day near Page. Glen Canyon Dam + lake views.

rows.activity: Glen Canyon Dam visitor center + views
rows.notes: Carl Hayden Visitor Center. Guided dam tours available. Good intro to the Colorado River system.

days.title: Horseshoe Bend + Antelope Canyon
days.theme: The two Page icons. Morning light for Horseshoe Bend, afternoon tour for Antelope.

rows.notes: Mile Marker 545, Highway 89. 20-min walk to overlook. Morning light best.
Pre-booked. Lower Antelope is less crowded than Upper. Allow 1 hr for the tour.

day_principles: Antelope Canyon MUST be pre-booked — no walk-ups allowed.
Morning light is best for Horseshoe Bend. Do it first.
Don't cram more into this day — Antelope Canyon ends late afternoon.

days.title: Lake Powell — Rainbow Bridge
days.theme: Full-day boat tour. Depart before 7 AM.

rows.notes: Check boat tour schedule — some depart as early as 07:00

rows.activity: Rainbow Bridge National Monument boat tour
rows.notes: Full day on the water. The bridge is 290 ft tall — largest natural bridge in the world. Pack food and sunscreen.

rows.activity: Return, dinner in Page, pack for Tusayan move tomorrow

day_principles: This is a full-day commitment — do not try to add other activities.
Book the boat tour in advance. Antelope Point Marina or Wahweap Marina.

days.title: Transit Day — Page to Tusayan
days.theme: Drive day. Arrive Tusayan, check in, rest.

rows.notes: 3.5 hr drive to Tusayan. No rushing.

rows.activity: Rest / brief Grand Canyon rim preview (optional)
rows.notes: Light afternoon walk if energy allows

rows.activity: Early dinner, sleep — big day tomorrow

day_principles: Transit days require rest. Do not schedule major activities on drive days.
Helicopter ride was considered but not executed — too disruptive to schedule.

days.title: Grand Canyon South Rim — Hermit Road
days.theme: The definitive South Rim day. Hermit Road all viewpoints.

rows.notes: Visitor center, park map, shuttle plan
Trailview → Maricopa → Powell → Hopi → Mohave → Pima → Hermits Rest. Take the free park shuttle westbound, walk back eastbound sections. Best light in late afternoon at Hopi and Mohave points.

rows.activity: Rest / Bright Angel area / rim walk
Dinner in Tusayan or El Tovar
rows.notes: El Tovar requires reservations but is the iconic Grand Canyon dining experience

day_principles: Hermit Road is the definitive South Rim experience — cover all viewpoints, not just the famous ones.
Late afternoon light at Hopi Point is the best photography window.
The free park shuttle is the right way to do Hermit Road — driving is slower.

days.title: Rim Trail Morning + Drive to Phoenix
days.theme: Short morning activity, then long drive to Phoenix airport.

rows.notes: Easy paved trail. Sunrise views. Stay east of Mather Point for fewer crowds.
No stops — straight shot to airport

day_principles: Rim Trail is perfect for departure morning — easy, no time pressure, near the exit.
Tusayan to Phoenix is 3.5 hrs minimum. Depart by 11 AM for a 4 PM flight.
Do NOT try to add Bright Angel Trail or a canyon descent on departure day.

claude_analysis.overall_grade: A — Pragmatic regional trip with excellent curation

key_principles_the_human_applied.description: The human treated this as a canyon country circuit: Page (Horseshoe Bend, Antelope Canyon, Lake Powell) then Grand Canyon. They spent MORE time in Page than at Grand Canyon. An AI generating 'Grand Canyon' itinerary will likely over-stuff the South Rim instead of seeing the regional context.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI will probably try to fill all 7 days with Grand Canyon content, leading to over-scheduling at the South Rim. The human's wisdom is that 1.5 days at the rim is enough — what makes the trip special is the Page area.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Guided tours are non-negotiable logistics, not preferences
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Antelope Canyon requires a Navajo guided tour — no walk-ups. Rainbow Bridge requires a full-day boat tour. These are not 'things to do' — they are logistical commitments that define the day's structure.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI planners treat guided tours as activities. They are actually scheduling anchors: Antelope Canyon defines the entire afternoon of Day 2, Rainbow Bridge defines the entire Day 3.

key_principles_the_human_applied.principle: Deliberate exclusion of famous but low-value experiences
key_principles_the_human_applied.description: West Rim Skywalk, Hoover Dam, North Rim, full Bright Angel Trail — all explicitly crossed out. The human made hard calls to exclude famous things that didn't fit the trip arc.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI boards include everything. The human knew that adding the West Rim Skywalk would consume a full day for an experience they deemed not worth it.

key_principles_the_human_applied.description: Day 4 (Page to Tusayan drive) has check-in and sleep. That's it. Even though the helicopter ride was considered, it didn't happen.
key_principles_the_human_applied.ai_gap: AI planners add activities to transit days. The human understood that a 3.5-hr drive + settling into a new base is a full day.

must_include_on_board: Bright Angel Trail or Rim Trail
ai_will_likely_miss: Lake Powell / Rainbow Bridge boat tour

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: South Rim content limited to 1.5–2 days. Not filling 4+ days purely at the rim.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: 4+ days of South Rim activities with no regional canyon country content.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Antelope Canyon noted as guided tour (pre-booking required). Any slot for Antelope Canyon has a realistic afternoon timing and a booking note.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Antelope Canyon listed as a walk-up activity with a 30-min slot.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Hermit Road scheduled as a multi-hour activity covering multiple viewpoints, not a single 30-min stop.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Hermit Road listed as a 30-min stop or combined with 3 other South Rim activities in the same morning.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Arrival day and any transit/drive day has minimal activities.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Drive day from Page to Tusayan includes major sightseeing.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: Departure day allows 3.5–4 hrs drive to Phoenix. No demanding activities.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Full canyon hike scheduled on departure morning.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: West Rim Skywalk not forced into itinerary. North Rim not included (correct — different access road).
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: West Rim Skywalk, Hoover Dam, and North Rim all scheduled on the same trip.

itinerary_scoring_dimensions.pass_criteria: At least a mention of Horseshoe Bend or Antelope Canyon as day-trip options from Page area.
itinerary_scoring_dimensions.fail_signal: Zero mention of Page/canyon country — purely South Rim content.
